SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct was SFTed on the SmolTalk instruction dataset using TRL plus the alignment-handbook recipe.

SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct was fine-tuned on the SmolTalk dataset, one of three new datasets the SmolLM2 team built (alongside FineMath and Stack-Edu) because existing instruction-following data was deemed too small or low-quality.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SmolLM3-3B (chat / dual instruct+reasoning) is initialized from SmolLM3-3B-Base after long-context extension and then post-trained via reasoning mid-training, SFT, APO alignment, and merging.
description_variants: SmolLM3-3B (chat / dual instruct+reasoning) is initialized from SmolLM3-3B-Base after long-context extension and then post-trained via reasoning mid-training, SFT, APO alignment, and merging.

SmolLM3-3B chat/instruct is initialized from the SmolLM3-3B-Base pretrained checkpoint and continues with midtraining on 140B reasoning tokens, supervised fine-tuning, and APO alignment to produce the released instruct model.

SmolLM3-3B (3B-parameter hybrid-reasoning instruct model) is initialized from the SmolLM3-3B-Base pretrain checkpoint and continued through midtraining on 140B reasoning tokens, supervised fine-tuning, and APO alignment.

SmolLM3-3B (the released instruct/reasoning model) inherits weights from the SmolLM3-3B-Base checkpoint via a cascade of long-context mid-training, reasoning mid-training, SFT, APO alignment, and final model merging.

SmolLM3-3B (final Instruct release) is initialized at the mid-training stage from HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-Base; the mid-trained checkpoint lives at HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-checkpoints (revision it-mid-training).

edges.description: The mid-training mixture is also released as part of HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2 (the 'mid' subset), per author confirmations in the blog comments.
description_variants: The mid-training mixture is also released as part of HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2 (the 'mid' subset), per author confirmations in the blog comments.

SmolLM3-3B's mid-training phase consumed the `Mid` config of smoltalk2 (4.78M rows, ~35.17B tokens) prior to SFT and preference optimization.

SFT phase of SmolLM3-3B consumed the `SFT` config of smoltalk2 (3.38M examples, ~19.29B tokens), an aggregate of 25 underlying datasets (10 think + 15 no_think) decontaminated against eval benchmarks.
Preference (APO) phase of SmolLM3-3B consumed the `Preference` config of smoltalk2 (446886 rows total).
Mid-training trains on the smoltalk2 'Mid' config — the Llama_Nemotron_Post_Training_Dataset_reasoning_r1 split and the OpenThoughts3_1.2M split, each at weight 1.0 — packing on, max_length 32768.

SmolLM3-3B SFT trains on the smoltalk2 'SFT' config across 25 named splits (everyday_convs_no_think, systemchats_30k_no_think, tulu_3_sft_personas_instruction_following_no_think, hermes_function_calling_v1_no_think, smol_magpie_ultra_no_think, multilingual_8languages_lang_5_no_think, table_gpt_no_think, OpenHermes_2.5_no_think, OpenThoughts3_1.2M_no_think_no_think, Mixture_of_Thoughts_science_no_think, explore_instruct_rewriting_no_think, smol_rewrite_no_think, smol_summarize_no_think, LongAlign_64k_context_lang_annotated_lang_6_no_think, multi_turn_reasoning_if_think, everyday_convs_reasoning_Qwen3_32B_think, systemchats_Qwen3_32B_think, xlam_traces_no_think, smolagents_toolcalling_traces_think, s1k_1.1_think, LongAlign_64k_Qwen3_32B_yarn_131k_think, aya_dataset_Qwen3_32B_think, multilingual8_Qwen3_32B_think, OpenThoughts3_1.2M_think, table_gpt_Qwen3_32B_think). Mixed weights 0.02–1.0; 5 epochs.
Final DPO/APO uses two preference splits of smoltalk2 'Preference' config (llama_3.1_tulu_3_8b_preference_mixture_no_think weight 0.5, tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think weight 0.25).

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: Qwen3-32B in reasoning (thinking) mode generated synthetic reasoning traces used in SFT for domains lacking such data (multi-turn conversations, multilinguality, everyday conversations); these synthetic completions form a portion of the 10 reasoning-mode SFT datasets.
description_variants: Qwen3-32B in reasoning (thinking) mode generated synthetic reasoning traces used in SFT for domains lacking such data (multi-turn conversations, multilinguality, everyday conversations); these synthetic completions form a portion of the 10 reasoning-mode SFT datasets.
Qwen3-32B generated the chosen completions for SmolLM3's reasoning-mode synthetic preference pairs used in APO.

Qwen3-32B generated the response/think traces for many SFT think splits in smoltalk2: smoltalk_multilingual8 (244736), smoltalk_everyday_convs_reasoning (2057), smoltalk_systemchats (27436), aya_dataset (15222), table_gpt (13201), LongAlign-64k-yarn-131k (7526), and Multi-Turn IF responses (28217). It is the dominant `_think` generator in the SFT mix.
Qwen3-32B generated the chosen completions (preferred responses) for the synthetic `tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think` split of smoltalk2 Preference (216385 rows, weight 0.25).

Qwen3-32B (in reasoning mode) was prompted with prompts from existing non-reasoning datasets to generate synthetic reasoning-mode SFT data covering multi-turn conversations, multilinguality, and everyday conversations — gaps the team could not otherwise fill from public reasoning datasets.
Qwen3-32B generations served as the 'chosen' completions in synthetic preference pairs for reasoning-mode APO alignment of SmolLM3-3B; pairs complement the Tulu3 non-reasoning data.

Several SFT splits in smoltalk2 SFT config carry traces generated by Qwen3-32B (thinking mode): smoltalk_everyday_convs_reasoning_Qwen3_32B_think, smoltalk_systemchats_Qwen3_32B_think, LongAlign_64k_Qwen3_32B_yarn_131k_think, aya_dataset_Qwen3_32B_think, smoltalk_multilingual8_Qwen3_32B_think, table_gpt_Qwen3_32B_think.
tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think split bundles preference pairs whose 'chosen' answers were generated by Qwen3-32B (thinking mode); used in SmolLM3-3B's DPO mixture.

description_variants: Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT is initialized from the Olmo-3-1025-7B base checkpoint and continues with supervised fine-tuning.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B
edges.description: The 'dolma1_7-wiki-en' top-level slug of the OLMo-mix-0625-official mix supplies the Dolma 1.7 English Wikipedia slice to Olmo 3 7B stage-1 pretraining.
description_variants: The 'dolma1_7-wiki-en' top-level slug of the OLMo-mix-0625-official mix supplies the Dolma 1.7 English Wikipedia slice to Olmo 3 7B stage-1 pretraining.
Per the aggregator+leaf rule: OLMo-3 7B base trained on the 'dolma1-7-wiki-en' sub-corpus of allenai/dolma3_mix-6T-1025-7B during Stage 1 pretraining (Dolma-1.7 English Wikipedia slice).
Per-leaf midtraining edge: OLMo-3-1025-7B's stage-2 mix sources wiki_to_rcqa: Wikipedia rewritten into reading-comprehension QA.

Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT lineage traces back to the Olmo 3 7B base model (allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B) via the Think SFT warm start.

SFT warm-starts from a reasoning-mid-trained checkpoint of the OLMo-3 7B base (gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/checkpoints/olmo3-7b-reasoning-sft-final), which is rooted in allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B.

The 7B Instruct SFT run warm-starts from the long-context Olmo-3 7B base checkpoint at gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/checkpoints/olmo3-7b-reasoning-sft-final/model_and_optim. The closest released ancestor in the lattice is allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B (Olmo-3 7B base release).

The 7B Instruct SFT run is initialized from the OLMo-3 7B base checkpoint (model_name=olmo3-7b in the OLMo-core SFT script). The local gs:// path points to the OLMo-3 base post-anneal/long-context checkpoint that becomes the SFT warm-start.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT
edges.description: SFT dataset for the Instruct family (math, code, chat, and general-knowledge queries); declared in the frontmatter datasets list and linked in the Stage-1 SFT body paragraph.
description_variants: SFT dataset for the Instruct family (math, code, chat, and general-knowledge queries); declared in the frontmatter datasets list and linked in the Stage-1 SFT body paragraph.

Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT is supervised-finetuned on the Dolci Instruct SFT dataset, built on top of the OLMo 2 Instruct mixture with improvements for chat, reasoning, and function-calling capabilities (Table 30 enumerates the prompt sources, totaling ~2.15M prompts).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised-fine-tuned on the 2,152,112-sample Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (aggregator dataset combining external prompt sources and new Ai2 sub-corpora).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was post-trained (SFT) on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT aggregator mixture totaling 2,152,112 prompts assembled by Ai2 from external corpora plus new Ai2 prompt sets (Tulu-3 Precise IF, Python Algorithms, logic puzzles, verifiable reasoning, hardcoded data, WildChat with GPT-4.1-upgraded responses, Dolci-Instruct-SFT-Tool-Use).
Aggregator dataset Dolci Instruct SFT, the full instruct SFT mixture (~2.15M prompts) curated from all sources in Table 30.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (Section 5.2.2).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is the SFT checkpoint trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples) that aggregates the constituent prompt sources enumerated below.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT is trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (~2.15M prompts) covering chat, IF, math, coding, safety, science, multilingual, and tool-use data (Table 30).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised-fine-tuned on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples spanning math, code, instruction-following, multilingual, science, safety, and tool-use prompts).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised fine-tuned on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mix (gs path 'rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114' is the same data tag used for OLMo-3 Instruct SFT).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised-fine-tuned on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture, a 2,152,112-sample ODC-BY licensed corpus comprising both repurposed prompts and new prompts authored by the OLMo team.

allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised fine-tuned on allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT (~2.15M filtered prompt-response pairs) covering chat, IF, math, code, safety, science, multilingual, function calling, and other domains.
SFT data path gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114 corresponds to the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture per the lattice description for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised fine-tuned on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples spanning multilingual chat, instruction following, tool use, code, math, and safety prompts).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT trained on the full Dolci-Instruct-SFT mix; the no-tools variant (1,924,533 samples) is a downstream republish of the same training data with tool-use rows excised, not a separate training event.

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is fine-tuned on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (~2,152,112 prompts in Table 30), spanning Chat, Precise IF, Math, Coding, Safety, Science, Multilingual, Tool Use and other categories.
The SFT run is fed the private GS dataset olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114, which is the internal preparation of the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture publicly released alongside the OLMo 3 paper.
SFT mixture is the OLMo-3 32B Instruct SFT dataset (rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114), released publicly as Dolci-Instruct-SFT for the OLMo-3 Instruct track.
allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is supervised-fine-tuned on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT aggregator (~2.15M prompts).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised fine-tuned on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples) — the umbrella post-training dataset that aggregates ~21 prompt sources for the Olmo-3 7B Instruct path.

Supervised fine-tuning data is the 32k packed rl-sft-32k mix at gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114; per the OLMo-3 paper this is released as allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT, the official OLMo-3 Instruct SFT mixture.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT aggregator mixture (2,152,112 multilingual SFT samples) released by Allen AI.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT trains on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture.
SFT supervised data is the rl-sft 32k mix at gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114, the internal preparation of the Dolci-Instruct-SFT dataset.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples) - the SFT-stage post-training mixture for the Instruct line of Olmo 3 7B.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT trains on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (~2.15M prompts; Table 30).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was trained on the full Dolci Instruct SFT aggregator mixture (2,152,112 samples, ODC-BY) covering math, code, instruction-following, multilingual, safety, science and tool-use prompts.

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is trained on the public Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (~2.15M samples spanning OpenThoughts 3, FLAN v2, Tulu 3 personas, WildChat, WildGuardMix, Aya, Dolci Tulu 3 Precise IF, etc.); the script loads it from the internal GS mirror at gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114.
Aggregator Dolci Instruct SFT used as the SFT mixture.

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was supervised fine-tuned on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture, an aggregate of 2,152,112 samples drawn from a curated set of public prompt sources plus new Ai2-authored prompts; counts are smaller than original sources due to extensive quality filtering and Azure API content filtering.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is trained on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (2,152,112 prompts spanning chat, math, code, science, safety, multilingual, IF, tool-use, and reasoning sources per Table 30).
SFT data: Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (declared in YAML frontmatter datasets and linked in body as 'Dolci-Instruct-SFT-7B' pointing to allenai/dolci-instruct-sft).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT is trained on the full Dolci Instruct SFT aggregate mixture of 2,152,112 multilingual ODC-BY prompts, which bundles the component sub-datasets enumerated in the same dataset card.
Aggregator: Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples) is the SFT training corpus for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT, composed of ~20 sub-corpora enumerated below.
Olmo 3 Instruct 7B SFT is trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture, an aggregator dataset whose composition is detailed in Table 30.
The 7B Instruct SFT is trained on the OLMo 3 Instruct SFT mix corresponding to the released Dolci-Instruct-SFT dataset; the script points at a private GCS prefix for this same mix.

Trained on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT dataset; the script references the working path gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114 which is the pre-release staging path for allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT.
Trained on the aggregated Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture, which is built on top of the OLMo 2 Instruct mixture and adds new function-calling, IF, and science data.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was trained on the full Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 prompts) — the released aggregator for the Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT stage.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT was post-trained (SFT) on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 samples).
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (2,152,112 prompts; Table 30).
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT trained on allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT (136,833 prompts in SFT mix; Table 30) — subset: Dolci Instruct Precise IF
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT trained on allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT (186,345 prompts in SFT mix; Table 30) — subset: Dolci Instruct Python Algorithms
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT trained on allenai/Dolci-Instruct-SFT (69 prompts in SFT mix; Table 30) — subset: Dolci Instruct Hardcoded
Dolci-Instruct-SFT (2,152,112-prompt SFT mixture) is the SFT training data for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT.

The SFT trainer reads its dataset from gs://ai2-llm/jacobm/data/sft/rl-sft-32k/olmo3-32b-instruct-sft-1114, which is the working corpus for the Olmo-3 instruct SFT family aggregated as Dolci-Instruct-SFT.

7B Instruct SFT trains on the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture (the public release counterpart of the rl-sft-32k Instruct SFT data referenced via dataset_path); per the lattice, Dolci-Instruct-SFT is the SFT mixture for OLMo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT.
Dolci Instruct SFT Mixture (2,152,112 samples, ODC-BY) is the SFT data for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT. Aggregator-level edge — see sibling events for component datasets.

Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is trained on the Dolci Instruct SFT Mixture, an SFT data aggregator that builds upon the OLMo 2 Instruct mixture and adds new chat, reasoning, function-calling, and science prompts (Table 30).
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT trains on the Dolci Instruct SFT Mixture (Allen AI's canonical instruct-SFT data for OLMo 3 Instruct).

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B
edges.description: The Dolma 3 fastText quality classifier filters the STEM-Heavy Crawl subset (threshold 0.6, top 2.83%) and selects the top two web quality buckets sampled into midtraining, gating which documents enter the Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix.
description_variants: The Dolma 3 fastText quality classifier filters the STEM-Heavy Crawl subset (threshold 0.6, top 2.83%) and selects the top two web quality buckets sampled into midtraining, gating which documents enter the Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix.

A fastText quality classifier (distilled from DCLM-style positives such as OpenHermes-2.5, ELI5, UltraChat-200k, WildChat-1M) assigns each web document a quality score, driving inclusion-based quality-aware upsampling for the Olmo 3 web pool.
The Dolma 3 fastText quality classifier scored every web document and bucketed them into 20 quality vigintiles, driving inclusion and upsampling decisions for the Olmo 3 7B pretraining mix.
Midtrain mix includes 'common_crawl-high-quality_vigintile_0018/0020_subset-decon-2_<topic>' rows: top-vigintile Common Crawl content selected by the Dolma 3 FastText quality classifier.
Olmo 3 Base 7B web pretraining data was filtered/scored by the dolma3 fastText quality classifier; quality scores partitioned the corpus into vigintile buckets used in quality-aware upsampling.
The dolma3 fasttext quality classifier was used (threshold 0.6) to filter STEM-Heavy Crawl documents prior to midtraining inclusion.
The dolma3 fasttext quality classifier (and topic classifier indirectly) generates the quality buckets that are sampled for the HQ Common Crawl subset.

The Dolma 3 fastText quality classifier (distilled from a transformer model trained with OpenHermes-2.5/UltraChat-200k/WildChat-1M positives and DCLM-RefinedWeb negatives) scored every CommonCrawl document for the quality-aware upsampling step.
STEM-Heavy Crawl midtraining sub-corpus consumed by allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B is filtered with the Dolma 3 fasttext quality classifier at threshold 0.6 (top 2.83%).

The fastText quality classifier was applied to the deduplicated Dolma 3 web corpus to assign quality scores driving the quality-aware upsampling and filtering in the pretraining mix used for Olmo-3-1025-7B.

Olmo 3 Base 7B's pretraining web corpus was filtered/scored by the dolma3 fastText quality classifier; quality scores within each topic partition determined vigintile buckets used for quality-aware upsampling.
Olmo 3 Base 7B's STEM-Heavy Crawl midtraining sub-corpus was filtered by the dolma3 fastText quality classifier with threshold 0.6 (top 2.83% of crawled data).
fastText quality classifier scored every deduplicated CommonCrawl document; quality-aware upsampling then favored higher-quality documents in the Dolma 3 Mix used to pretrain Olmo 3 7B Base.

Documents in the Dolma 3 web pool (later sampled into both pretraining and the midtraining HQ web/STEM-heavy subsets used by allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B) were scored by the Dolma 3 FastText quality classifier for quality-aware upsampling and filtering.

A FastText-based quality classifier (distilled from WebOrganizer-style models) assigns each pretraining document a quality score, used for quality-aware upsampling rather than flat filtering. Negatives drawn from DCLM-RefinedWeb; positives from OpenHermes-2.5, ELI5, UltraChat-200k, WildChat-1M.

edges.description: The dolmino-1-flan subset (5B tokens) reuses the FLAN subset and preprocessing from OLMo 2 Dolmino Mix because microanneals showed Flan improves QA-task performance in midtraining.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT
edges.description: DeepSeek-R1 generated reasoning traces and completions for chat and safety prompts (Tulu 3 / WildChat / OpenAssistant subsets) and for additional non-math/non-code data sources (Aya, TableGPT) that populate Dolci-Think-SFT-7B used to train Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT.
description_variants: DeepSeek-R1 generated reasoning traces and completions for chat and safety prompts (Tulu 3 / WildChat / OpenAssistant subsets) and for additional non-math/non-code data sources (Aya, TableGPT) that populate Dolci-Think-SFT-7B used to train Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT.

DeepSeek-R1 produced new reasoning traces over the existing prompts repurposed from Tülu 3 / OLMo 2 (WildChat, oasst1, CoCoNot, WildGuardMix, WildJailbreak, Aya, TableGPT, Olmo identity) that populate Dolci-Think-SFT-7B.

DeepSeek-R1 generated new reasoning traces for the existing-prompt portions of the 7B Dolci-Think-SFT mix repurposed from Tulu 3/OLMo 2 (WildChat, oasst1, CoCoNot, WildGuardMix, WildJailbreak, Aya, TableGPT).
DeepSeek-R1 generates reasoning traces and completions for the Chat & safety prompts (Tülu-3 WildChat/OpenAssistant subsets) and other non-math/code datasets in Think SFT 7B.
For chat & safety prompts, reasoning traces and completions in Dolci-Think-SFT-7B are generated by DeepSeek R1.
Reasoning-chain completions for the science/other (TableGPT, Aya, etc.) portions of Dolci-Think-SFT-7B are generated by DeepSeek R1.

DeepSeek R1 generated new <think>...</think> reasoning traces for the existing Tülu 3 / OLMo 2 prompts (WildChat, oasst1, CoCoNot, WildGuardMix, WildJailbreak, Aya, TableGPT, Olmo Identity Prompts) that compose part of the Dolci-Think-SFT-7B SFT mixture.

DeepSeek R1 generated reasoning traces and completions for the chat, safety, science, and 'other' (TableGPT, Aya) prompts in allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT's SFT mix.
DeepSeek R1 generates reasoning traces and completions for the chat, safety, TableGPT, and Aya components of Dolci-Think-SFT-7B (Section 4.2.1).

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT
edges.description: OpenThoughts3 (Guha et al., 2025a) contributes the math, STEM, code, and science subsets that populate Dolci Think OpenThoughts 3+ Math/STEM/Code; incomplete OpenThoughts3 reasoning traces are regenerated by QwQ-32B (math/code) or DeepSeek-R1 (other) before inclusion in Dolci-Think-SFT-7B.
description_variants: OpenThoughts3 (Guha et al., 2025a) contributes the math, STEM, code, and science subsets that populate Dolci Think OpenThoughts 3+ Math/STEM/Code; incomplete OpenThoughts3 reasoning traces are regenerated by QwQ-32B (math/code) or DeepSeek-R1 (other) before inclusion in Dolci-Think-SFT-7B.
OpenThoughts 3 supplied 941,166 prompts (extended to 32K context length and with code prompts downsampled to a 16x multiple) as the largest existing-reasoning-trace component of Dolci-Think-SFT-7B.

OpenThoughts 3 contributes existing reasoning traces (Apache 2.0); for the 7B mix it was extended to 32K context and code prompts downsampled 16x, yielding 941,166 prompts (the team's 'Dolci OpenThoughts 3' redistribution).
OpenThoughts3 math (752,997), STEM (99,269) and code (88,900) prompts (with regenerated reasoning chains) are included as Math/Science/Coding prompt sources for Think SFT 7B.

OpenThoughts 3 (1.2M reasoning traces) was extended to 32K context and code prompts downsampled 16x, yielding 941,166 prompts ('Dolci OpenThoughts 3') used in the Dolci-Think-SFT-7B mixture for Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT.

OpenThoughts 3 reasoning traces extended to 32K context, code prompts downsampled to 16X. Included as a leaf component of the allenai/Dolci-Think-SFT-7B SFT mixture used for allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT.
allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT's SFT mix uses OpenThoughts3 math/STEM/code subsets (752K + 99K + 88K prompts, upsampled).
Dolci Think OpenThoughts 3+ Math (752,997), STEM (99,269), Code (88,900), and Science subsets all derive from OpenThoughts3 (Guha et al., 2025a) per Table 17.

OpenThoughts3-1.2M (Apache 2.0) is incorporated into Dolci-Think-SFT-7B after being extended to 32K context length and downsampling code prompts to a 16x multiple, yielding 941,166 prompts (released as 'Dolci OpenThoughts 3').
Trained on the math subset of OpenThoughts3-1.2M (752,997 prompts; '+' indicates regenerated/filtered traces) used as Dolci Think OpenThoughts3+ Math.
Trained on the STEM subset of OpenThoughts3-1.2M (99,269 prompts) as Dolci Think OpenThoughts3+ STEM.
Trained on the code subset of OpenThoughts3-1.2M (88,900 prompts) as Dolci Think OpenThoughts3+ Code; downsampled to 16 repetitions and incomplete completions regenerated.

Dolci-Think-SFT-7B incorporates OpenThoughts3-1.2M (Apache 2.0), extended to 32K context length and downsampled code prompts to 16X multiple, totaling 941,166 prompts (Ai2's 'Dolci OpenThoughts 3' version).

OpenThoughts 3 (open-thoughts/OpenThoughts3-1.2M, Apache 2.0) supplied existing reasoning traces; Ai2 extended it to 32K context and downsampled code prompts to a 16x multiple, yielding 941,166 total prompts (released as the Dolci OpenThoughts 3 derivative).
Math, code, STEM, and science prompts (with regenerated reasoning traces) sourced from OpenThoughts3.
OpenThoughts 3 reasoning traces (941,166 prompts after extending to 32K context and downsampling code prompts to 16x) are repackaged as 'Dolci OpenThoughts 3' inside the Dolci-Think-SFT-7B mixture.

OpenThoughts3-1.2M contributes 941,166 reasoning prompts, extended to 32K context length and with code prompts downsampled to 16X multiple; the Olmo-modified version is shipped as Dolci OpenThoughts 3.

Dolci Think SFT 7B includes 752,997 OpenThoughts3 math prompts plus 99,269 OpenThoughts3 STEM and 88,900 OpenThoughts3 code prompts (free-text Dolci Think OpenThoughts 3+ subsets), as well as the OpenThoughts3 science subset for the science slice.
OpenThoughts 3 (Apache 2.0) extended to 32K context length with code prompts downsampled to a 16x multiple, contributing 941,166 prompts (the team's redistribution is named 'Dolci OpenThoughts 3').
Math, STEM and Code leaves in Dolci Think SFT 7B: 752,997 OpenThoughts3+ Math, 99,269 OpenThoughts3+ STEM, and 88,900 OpenThoughts3+ Code prompts.

OpenThoughts 3 reasoning traces (Apache 2.0) extended to 32K context length with code prompts downsampled 16x, totaling 941,166 prompts, included in Dolci-Think-SFT-7B as a source of existing reasoning traces. The Ai2-modified version is referred to as 'Dolci OpenThoughts 3'.

OpenThoughts 3 (Apache 2.0) is included in the Dolci-Think-SFT-7B mixture, extended to 32K context length and with code prompts downsampled to a 16x multiple, totaling 941,166 prompts (the in-house transformed version is named Dolci OpenThoughts 3).

Component dataset within Dolci-Think-SFT-7B: OpenThoughts 3 reasoning traces extended to 32K context length with code prompts downsampled to a 16x multiple, yielding 941,166 prompts that were then included in the SFT mixture.
OpenThoughts3 reasoning-trace corpus extended to 32K context length and downsampled with code prompts at a 16x multiple, contributing 941,166 prompts ('Dolci OpenThoughts 3') to the 7B SFT mix.
Math subset (Dolci Think OpenThoughts 3+ Math, 752,997 prompts) is sourced from the math subset of OpenThoughts3 (Guha et al., 2025a); for incomplete traces QwQ-32B regenerates the reasoning chain.
STEM subset of OpenThoughts3 (Dolci Think OpenThoughts 3+ STEM, 99,269 prompts; Table 17) — sourced from OpenThoughts3 science subset. Subset of OpenThoughts3-1.2M.
Code subset of OpenThoughts3 (Dolci Think OpenThoughts 3+ Code, 88,900 prompts; Table 17). Subset of OpenThoughts3-1.2M; combined with Dolci Think Python Algorithms.
Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT trained on OpenThoughts3-1.2M (Apache 2.0) extended to 32K context with code prompts downsampled 16x to 941,166 prompts — released as the Dolci OpenThoughts 3 variant.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT
edges.description: Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 7B Think-SFT checkpoint to give the Instruct training a 'warm-start'; Table 29 reports a +3.3 average-point gain for Instruct SFT trained with thinking SFT first vs without.
description_variants: Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 7B Think-SFT checkpoint to give the Instruct training a 'warm-start'; Table 29 reports a +3.3 average-point gain for Instruct SFT trained with thinking SFT first vs without.
Olmo 3 Instruct SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 Think SFT checkpoint to provide a warm-start; this significantly improves Instruct performance per Table 29.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 7B Think-SFT checkpoint as a 'warm-start'. The team found this significantly improves performance (Table 29).
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is initialized from Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT (warm-start) before SFT on Dolci-Instruct-SFT; this yields significant gains over training from base.
Per Section 5.2.2 and Figure 2, the Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT model is initialized from the Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT checkpoint (warm-start) before SFT on Dolci-Instruct-SFT.

Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is warm-started from Olmo 3 7B Think SFT, not from Olmo 3 Base directly. Section 5.5 reports this strategy yields gains across benchmarks while keeping responses succinct without remnants of thinking traces.
Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT warm-starts from the Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT checkpoint (Sec 5.2.2).
Olmo 3 Instruct 7B SFT initializes weights from Olmo 3 Think SFT 7B checkpoint as a thinking-warm-start.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT is initialized from the Olmo 3 7B Think SFT checkpoint (warm-start), which significantly improves Instruct performance per Table 29.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT initializes from Olmo 3 7B Think SFT for warm-start.
Olmo 3 Instruct 7B SFT is warm-started from the Olmo 3 Think 7B SFT checkpoint, which the paper reports yields significant Instruct-stage performance gains.
OLMo 3 Instruct SFT for the 7B variant warm-starts from the Olmo 3 Think SFT checkpoint at the same scale, which significantly improves Instruct benchmark scores (Table 29).
Olmo 3 7B Instruct SFT warm-starts from Olmo 3 7B Think SFT (§5.2.2). Table 29 shows With-thinking-SFT-first averages 47.8 vs 44.5 No-thinking-SFT.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT
edges.description: WildChat (302,406 prompts in Table 30) is a Chat & Precise IF source dataset feeding the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture used to SFT Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT.
description_variants: WildChat (302,406 prompts in Table 30) is a Chat & Precise IF source dataset feeding the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture used to SFT Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT.

302,406 WildChat prompts (Zhao et al., 2024) were incorporated into the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture; the prompts are the user-side WildChat data while the responses were re-generated by GPT-4.1 (see the generated_by edge). Source pins WildChat at the family/concept level without specifying a size variant, so the address resolves to the WildChat root.
WildChat (302,406 prompts) is the largest chat-style prompt source in the Dolci Instruct SFT mix.
Chat & WildChat: 302,406 prompts in SFT mix from WildChat (Zhao et al., 2024a).

Constituent of the Dolci-Instruct-SFT mixture: WildChat prompts (302,406; ODC-BY-1.0; Zhao et al., 2024) used with upgraded responses generated by GPT-4.1; source does not pin a WildChat release version, so addressed at the WildChat family root.
Olmo 3 7B Instruct-SFT uses 302,406 WildChat prompts as the chat-data backbone of Dolci Instruct SFT (Table 30).

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-SFT used 302,406 prompts from WildChat as a sub-corpus of the Dolci Instruct SFT mixture (WildChat prompts whose responses were upgraded by GPT-4.1; Zhao et al. 2024; family-root pin (card does not name a specific WildChat HF leaf)).

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-32B-Think
edges.description: Olmo 3 Think 32B (final, 750-step OlmoRL) is initialized from Olmo 3 Think 32B DPO and trained on Dolci-Think-RL-32B.
description_variants: Olmo 3 Think 32B (final, 750-step OlmoRL) is initialized from Olmo 3 Think 32B DPO and trained on Dolci-Think-RL-32B.
Olmo-3-32B-Think is initialized from the Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO checkpoint and continues training with reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) on the Dolci Think RL mixture.
Olmo 3 Think 32B is RLVR-trained from the Think DPO 32B checkpoint (final RL run on top of the DPO checkpoint).
Olmo 3 32B Think (final 3.0) is initialized from Olmo 3 32B Think DPO and trained with OlmoRL for 750 steps. Initial training run; 32B uses the 7B-DPO-filtered prompt mix due to compute constraints.
Olmo-3-32B-Think (final RLVR-trained checkpoint) is initialized from Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO and continues with RLVR training on the Dolci-Think-RL-32B prompt mixture.
Olmo 3 32B Think (initial RL release) is initialized from Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO; OlmoRL training proceeds for ~5 days on 224 GPUs with 8 H100 nodes for training and 20 nodes for inference.
Olmo-3-32B-Think (Final Models RLVR row) continues from the Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO checkpoint per the Model Family table - RLVR is the post-training stage immediately after DPO in the 32B-Think column.
Olmo-3-32B-Think initializes from Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO and is finetuned via OlmoRL (Section 4.4.3, Table 24).
The final 32B Think 3.0 model is initialized from the Think-DPO 32B checkpoint and trained with OlmoRL.
Olmo-3-32B-Think (final RLVR) continues from Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO with an RLVR stage.
32B Think (RLVR 3.0) continues from the 32B Think DPO checkpoint via OlmoRL on Dolci-Think-RL-32B.
Olmo-3-32B-Think (final RLVR model) is initialized from Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO per the Olmo-3 family staging (Base → SFT → DPO → RLVR).
The final Olmo-3-32B-Think (RLVR) checkpoint continues training from the Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO checkpoint per the Model Family table layout (RLVR row follows DPO row in the same 32B Think column).
Final 32B Think model continues RL from the Think-DPO 32B checkpoint.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3.1-32B-Think
edges.description: Olmo 3.1 Think 32B is the extended-RL continuation of the same OlmoRL run for Olmo-3-32B-Think (initialized from Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO), trained for an additional ~21 days up to 2300 steps and yielding ~4+ point gains on AIME, ZebraLogic, IFEval and ~20 points on IFBench (Section 4.1.2 / Table 14).
description_variants: Olmo 3.1 Think 32B is the extended-RL continuation of the same OlmoRL run for Olmo-3-32B-Think (initialized from Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO), trained for an additional ~21 days up to 2300 steps and yielding ~4+ point gains on AIME, ZebraLogic, IFEval and ~20 points on IFBench (Section 4.1.2 / Table 14).

Olmo-3.1-32B-Think is a continuation of the SAME RL run that produced Olmo-3-32B-Think, which itself initialized from Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO; therefore Olmo-3.1-32B-Think also derives from the 32B Think DPO checkpoint.
Olmo-3.1-32B-Think continues the same RL run from the same Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO starting checkpoint, just for more epochs.
Olmo 3.1 32B Think is initialized from Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO, the same DPO checkpoint as Olmo 3 32B Think; the 3.1 release extends OlmoRL training to about 2,300 steps.

anchor_list.excerpt: Olmo 3.1 Think 32B to illustrate that extended OlmoRL training, via additional epochs on our Dolci Think RL dataset, leads to improved performance ... going up to 2300 steps for Olmo 3.1 Think 32B
anchor_list.explanation: Olmo 3.1 Think 32B continues the same OlmoRL run (initialized from the 32B Think-DPO checkpoint) with additional epochs.

anchor_list.excerpt: While Olmo 3 Think 32B was trained for 750 steps, we continued the run past our initial release, going up to 2300 steps for Olmo 3.1 Think 32B.
anchor_list.explanation: Same RL run, just extended; starting checkpoint is the same DPO checkpoint.

anchor_list.excerpt: While Olmo 3 Think 32B was trained for 750 steps, we continued the run past our initial release, going up to 2300 steps for Olmo 3.1 Think 32B.
anchor_list.explanation: Same starting checkpoint as Think 3.0; continued training.

anchor_list.excerpt: we continued the run past our initial release, going up to 2300 steps for Olmo 3.1 Think 32B
anchor_list.explanation: 3.1 Think extends the 32B Think RL run rather than restarting; therefore both build on Olmo-3-32B-Think-DPO.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO
edges.description: Qwen3 32B (thinking mode off) generates the chosen completions in the delta-learning heuristic preference pairs of Dolci Instruct DPO; it also serves as the larger generator for one variant of the multi-turn synthetic preferences.
description_variants: Qwen3 32B (thinking mode off) generates the chosen completions in the delta-learning heuristic preference pairs of Dolci Instruct DPO; it also serves as the larger generator for one variant of the multi-turn synthetic preferences.

Qwen3-32B contributed responses to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool in BOTH no-reasoning ('qwen3-no_reasoning-32b', 2,500,075 responses) and reasoning ('qwen3-reasoning-32b', 2,460,620 responses) modes.

Qwen/Qwen3-32B contributed responses (chosen and/or rejected) to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool from which allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO preference pairs were drawn (source config: qwen3-{no_reasoning,reasoning}-32b configs (both thinking modes)).
Delta-learning heuristic chosen responses are generated by Qwen3 32B (thinking off) following Geng et al. (2025); chosen pairs in the multi-turn variant also come from Qwen3 32B alongside GPT-4o.
Chosen responses in Dolci Instruct DPO delta-learning pairs (used to train 7B Instruct-DPO) are generated by Qwen3-32B with thinking off (Section 5.3.1).

Qwen/Qwen3-32B contributed responses to the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool (configs: qwen3-no_reasoning-32b, qwen3-reasoning-32b); these responses were filtered into chosen/rejected pairs in Dolci-Instruct-DPO and so generated training data for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO. Qwen3-32B (no-reasoning mode) generated up to 2.50M responses; Qwen3-32B (reasoning/thinking mode) generated up to 2.46M responses; same checkpoint as the no-reasoning entry, run with thinking enabled.

This generator's responses to ~2.5M shared prompts populate the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool, from which chosen/rejected completions for Dolci-Instruct-DPO (the DPO training mixture for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO) were drawn. Pool configs 'qwen3-no_reasoning-32b' and 'qwen3-reasoning-32b' (same lattice leaf, two inference modes).
Qwen3-32B (thinking mode OFF) generated chosen-completion candidates for the delta-learning heuristic preference pairs in allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO data.

Qwen/Qwen3-32B (both reasoning and no-reasoning sampling configurations) is a generator in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool (config 'qwen3-no_reasoning-32b / qwen3-reasoning-32b'); produced up to ~2.5M chat responses to Dolci-Instruct-SFT and WildChat prompts that fed into Dolci-Instruct-DPO chosen/rejected pair construction for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO preference tuning.
Qwen3-32B (no-thinking) generated the chosen completions in the delta-learning heuristic subset of Dolci-Instruct-DPO.
Qwen3-32B contributed sampled completions to the prompt pool whose outputs are judged by GPT-4.1 to form the delta-aware GPT-judged subset of Dolci-Instruct-DPO.
Qwen3-32B (thinking off) generates chosen responses for the delta-learning heuristic preference pairs used to train allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen3 32B (no-reasoning mode) (config 'qwen3-no_reasoning-32b') generated up to 2,500,075 candidate responses for the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool, which provided chosen/rejected candidates for the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference pairs used to train Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen3 32B (reasoning/thinking mode) (config 'qwen3-reasoning-32b') generated up to 2,460,620 candidate responses for the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool, which provided chosen/rejected candidates for the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference pairs used to train Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.
Qwen3 32B in no_reasoning mode generated ~2,500,075 responses across the prompt pool.
Qwen3 32B in reasoning mode generated ~2,460,620 responses across the prompt pool.
Qwen3-32B (no-thinking) generates the chosen completions in delta-learning heuristic pairs for Dolci Instruct DPO.
Qwen3-32B (no reasoning) generates DPO-pair completions in addition to delta-learning heuristic pairs.

Qwen3 32B sampled in both no-reasoning and reasoning modes produced responses (config qwen3-no_reasoning-32b + qwen3-reasoning-32b) in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool; these responses became chosen/rejected candidates assembled into the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference mixture used to preference-tune Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen3-32B with thinking mode OFF generates the chosen completions for Dolci Instruct DPO's delta-learning heuristic pairs (large-model chosen vs small-model rejected); also used as a chosen-side generator for one variant of multi-turn synthetic pairs.

Qwen3 32B used in both no_reasoning and reasoning (thinking) modes; same checkpoint contributed responses (~2,500,075 (no_reasoning) + 2,460,620 (reasoning) per inference mode) to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool (config 'qwen3-no_reasoning-32b / qwen3-reasoning-32b'); these responses became chosen/rejected candidates in the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference pairs that DPO-trained Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen3-32B generated the chosen completions in the related-query / paraphrase / repeat / self-talk Qwen-pair sub-corpora of the Instruct-DPO mixture (allenai/related-query_qwen_pairs_filtered_lbc100 etc.).
Qwen3-32B generates chosen responses for delta-learning heuristic pairs.

Qwen/Qwen3-32B generated candidate completions (2,500,075 (no-reasoning) + 2,460,620 (reasoning) responses, config `qwen3-no_reasoning-32b/qwen3-reasoning-32b`) that became part of the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool from which allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's preference pairs were drawn. Role: Qwen3 32B in both no-reasoning and reasoning modes (`qwen3-no_reasoning-32b` and `qwen3-reasoning-32b` configs).

Qwen3 32B (configs qwen3-no_reasoning-32b and qwen3-reasoning-32b) generated responses in the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool, likely contributing strong chosen-candidate responses (in thinking mode) for the Delta-Learning recipe.

(Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO consumes Dolci-Instruct-DPO.) Chosen responses for the delta-learning heuristic preference pairs in Dolci-Instruct-DPO are generated by Qwen3 32B in non-thinking mode (~125K pairs).
(Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO consumes Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool.) Qwen3-32B is one of the DPO response pool generators.

Qwen3-32B in no-reasoning mode (qwen3-no_reasoning-32b config, ~2.50M responses) is one of 29 generator models populating the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool feeding Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO preference pairs.

Qwen3-32B in reasoning (thinking-on) mode (qwen3-reasoning-32b config, ~2.46M responses) is one of 29 generator models populating the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool feeding Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO preference pairs.

Qwen3 32B (qwen3-no_reasoning-32b and qwen3-reasoning-32b configs; both thinking modes) generated prompt-response pairs in the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool that feeds Dolci-Instruct-DPO DPO training of Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.
Delta-learning heuristic chosen responses for Dolci Instruct DPO are generated by Qwen3 32B (thinking mode off).

Qwen3-32B used in BOTH no-reasoning and reasoning modes: contributed responses to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool (config 'qwen3-{no_reasoning,reasoning}-32b', ~2.5M responses), which is the candidate-response pool for OLMo 3 DPO preference pairs feeding the Dolci-Instruct-DPO mixture trained by Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

Qwen3-32B (both no-reasoning and reasoning/thinking configurations) contributed responses to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool used to construct Delta-Learning preference pairs for Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO; ~2.50M no-reasoning + ~2.46M reasoning responses.
Chosen completions in the delta-learning heuristic DPO pairs are generated by Qwen3 32B (no-thinking) — these pairs are used to train Olmo 3 7B Instruct DPO (§5.3.1).

Producer model Qwen/Qwen3-32B (config slug 'qwen3-no_reasoning-32b+qwen3-reasoning-32b') generated up to ~2.5M responses included in the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool, which underlies allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO training data. Mode: both reasoning and non-reasoning configs.
Delta-learning heuristic preference pairs are constructed by generating chosen responses with Qwen3-32B (thinking off) and rejected responses with Qwen3-0.6B, following Geng et al. (2025).

Qwen3-32B is one of the models in the completion pool used to generate candidate responses for the GPT-4.1-judged subset of Dolci Instruct DPO preference pairs (A.7.4 model pool for LLM-judged DPO preference pairs).

Qwen3-32B contributed responses to the response pool in both reasoning and no-reasoning modes. Selected responses became chosen or rejected completions in the Dolci Instruct DPO Mixture used to preference-tune Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.
Chosen responses for delta-learning heuristic preference pairs are generated by Qwen3-32B with thinking mode off (large-model side of the delta).

Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B contributed responses (chosen and/or rejected) to the Dolci-DPO-Model-Response-Pool from which allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO preference pairs were drawn (source config: qwen3-no_reasoning-0.6b config).
Delta-learning heuristic rejected responses are generated by Qwen3 0.6B (thinking off); rejected pairs in the multi-turn variant likewise.

Rejected completions in the delta-learning heuristic DPO pairs are generated by Qwen3 0.6B (no-thinking) — these pairs are used to train Olmo 3 7B Instruct DPO (§5.3.1).

Delta-aware GPT-judged DPO pipeline (§A.7.4) samples completions from a pool of weaker/previous-generation models, including Qwen3 0.6B (no thinking), to ensure low-quality rejected responses; these pairs train Olmo 3 7B Instruct DPO.

Producer model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B (config slug 'qwen3-no_reasoning-0.6b') generated up to ~2.5M responses included in the Dolci DPO Model Response Pool, which underlies allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's DPO training data. Mode: non-reasoning mode.
Qwen3-0.6B (thinking off) generates the rejected responses in delta-learning heuristic preference pairs, paired against Qwen3-32B chosen responses.

Qwen3-0.6B is one of the models in the completion pool used to generate candidate responses for the GPT-4.1-judged subset of Dolci Instruct DPO preference pairs (A.7.4 model pool for LLM-judged DPO preference pairs).

Qwen3-0.6B contributed no-reasoning-mode responses to the response pool. Selected responses became chosen or rejected completions in the Dolci Instruct DPO Mixture used to preference-tune Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO.

anchor_list.excerpt: Our initial attempts to modernize the UltraFeedback pipeline from OLMo 2 and Tülu 3 by improving the quality of the LLM judge (GPT-4o → GPT-4.1) ... For each prompt, we sample four model completions and judge them via a GPT-4.1 judge with the UltraFeedback judge prompts ... we enforce our judge pipeline to sample responses from exactly two of the following smaller and/or previous generation models which show lower overall performance: OLMo 2-1B-Instruct, OLMo 2-7B-Instruct, Yi-9B, Yi-34B, Phi4-Mini-Instruct, Qwen3-0.6B (no reasoning), Qwen3-1.7B (no reasoning).

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think
edges.description: Qwen3 32B (thinking mode OFF) hosted via vLLM is the LM judge that scores chat-reference and chat-open-ended responses in [0,1] during OlmoRL training of Olmo-3-7B-Think (general-chat reward). The judge prompts are given a max input of 32768 tokens and a 2048-token response budget.
description_variants: Qwen3-32B (thinking mode off, hosted via vLLM) is the default LM-as-a-judge that scores chat responses for Olmo 3 Think 7B RLVR rewards.
During 7B OlmoRL, Qwen3-32B (thinking mode off) acts as the LM judge for non-verifiable general-chat rewards (with-reference and open-ended), scoring responses on a 1-10 scale.
Qwen3-32B serves as the LLM judge model evaluating responses during GRPO RLVR for OLMo-3-7B-Think (reward signal aggregator alongside verifiable rewards).
Qwen/Qwen3-32B is the hosted LLM judge model used to score/verify rollouts during RL.

During OlmoRL training of Olmo 3 7B Think, Qwen3-32B (thinking mode off) acts as the LM judge that scores chat-reference and chat-open-ended completions [0,1], shaping the per-step reward signal for non-verifiable tasks.
During RL training of allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think, Qwen3-32B is used as the LLM-judge to score thinker rollouts on verifiable rewards.
Qwen3-32B (thinking mode OFF) is hosted as the LM judge for general-chat reward signals during allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think's OlmoRL training, scoring open-ended responses on a [0,1] scale.
Qwen3-32B is used as the LLM judge for verifiable-reward RL rollouts; it scores rollouts whose rewards then drive policy updates (filter on inclusion of correct/preferred trajectories).
Qwen3-32B serves as the LM judge for verifiable-reward RL rollouts in the no-pipeline run.
Qwen3-32B is hosted via vLLM (HOSTED_VLLM_API_BASE) as the LLM judge inside GRPO-Fast.
Qwen3-32B is again the hosted-vLLM LLM judge in this ablation.
Qwen3-32B (thinking mode off) acts as the LM judge that scores non-verifiable chat completions during RL training, gating which rollouts receive positive reward.
Qwen3-32B is configured as the LLM judge (--llm_judge_model hosted_vllm/Qwen/Qwen3-32B) used during GRPO to score non-verifiable reasoning rollouts.
Qwen3-32B is configured as the LLM judge (--llm_judge_model hosted_vllm/Qwen/Qwen3-32B) used during GRPO to score non-verifiable rollouts in the no-pipeline variant.
Qwen3-32B (hosted via vLLM) acts as the LLM judge for general/IF prompts during GRPO rollouts on the Think RL run.
Qwen3-32B (thinking off) is the default LM-judge that scores chat-reference / chat-open-ended trajectories during 7B OlmoRL training, gating reward.
Qwen3-32B is the LLM judge for non-verifiable rewards in this RLVR run; it scores rollouts and decides which pass the judge-based criteria (timeout 600s, max_tokens 2048, max_context 32768).
Qwen3-32B is the LLM judge for non-verifiable rewards; scores rollouts and decides which pass the judge-based criteria.
Qwen3-32B serves as the LM judge for general-chat reward in OlmoRL Think 7B.

Qwen3-32B is the hosted vLLM judge model used to score and filter rollouts in the GRPO RL loop.
Qwen3-32B is used as the LLM judge for the verifiable reward in GRPO.
Qwen3-32B (with thinking mode off) serves as the LM judge providing non-verifiable reward signals during OlmoRL training.
Qwen3-32B (hosted via vLLM) judges non-verifiable rollouts during GRPO RLVR, the OLMo-3 default RLVR LM judge.
Qwen3-32B (thinking mode off) is the LM judge providing the general-chat reward (reference and open-ended) used during OlmoRL training of Olmo-3-7B-Think.
Non-verifiable chat (reference and open-ended) rewards in the OlmoRL run for Olmo 3 7B Think were scored by Qwen3-32B hosted with thinking mode turned off via vLLM.
Qwen3-32B (thinking off) acted as LM judge providing the non-verifiable Chat-reference and Chat-open-ended reward scores during OlmoRL training of Olmo-3-7B-Think.

Qwen3-32B is the LLM judge for verifiable-reward decisions during the no-pipeline GRPO RLVR run.
Qwen/Qwen3-32B is configured as the LLM judge for the RL run via hosted_vllm/Qwen/Qwen3-32B, scoring rollouts to produce reward signal.
Qwen/Qwen3-32B is the LLM judge for the released RL run (no-pipeline script).

For chat (open-ended and reference-based) verification during RL, the team uses an LM-judge by default: Qwen3-32B with thinking off, hosted on vLLM, with up to 32K input and 2K response tokens. Footnote 36 makes this the global default LM-judge across all OLMo-3 RLVR runs.
Qwen3-32B serves as the LLM judge model that scores generations during RL, deciding reward signal for the GRPO update.

Olmo 3 7B Think DPO checkpoint is used as an offline-difficulty filter for Dolci-Think-RL-7B prompts: 8 rollouts per prompt at temperature 1.0 / top-p 1.0; prompts with pass-rate > 62.5% are removed from the RL training pool.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO
edges.description: Half of the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference pairs (125K) were generated using a delta-aware Ultrafeedback-esque GPT-judge pipeline, borrowing the Ultrafeedback methodology of LLM-judge-driven preference scoring. No Ultrafeedback weights or data are reused; the recipe is the borrowed element.
description_variants: Half of the Dolci-Instruct-DPO preference pairs (125K) were generated using a delta-aware Ultrafeedback-esque GPT-judge pipeline, borrowing the Ultrafeedback methodology of LLM-judge-driven preference scoring. No Ultrafeedback weights or data are reused; the recipe is the borrowed element.
Delta-aware GPT-judged DPO pipeline modernises the UltraFeedback pipeline used by OLMo 2 / Tulu 3, upgrading the judge to GPT-4.1 and adding delta-maximising interventions.

Half of the DPO pairs (125K) were built with a delta-aware 'Ultrafeedback-esque' GPT-judge pipeline — methodology borrows the UltraFeedback pairwise-judge preference-construction recipe but adds a delta-aware contrast objective.

Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO's preference data borrows the UltraFeedback GPT-judge methodology: 125K of its 260K pairs were built with a 'delta-aware Ultrafeedback-esque GPT-judge pipeline' that maximizes contrast between chosen and rejected responses.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-Hybrid-7B
edges.description: Hybrid-7B architecture is based on OLMo3 7B (TransformerConfig.olmo3_7B) with reduced attention heads and 3 GDN layers per 1 attention layer to match params/TPS for fair comparison.
description_variants: Hybrid-7B architecture is based on OLMo3 7B (TransformerConfig.olmo3_7B) with reduced attention heads and 3 GDN layers per 1 attention layer to match params/TPS for fair comparison.

Olmo-Hybrid-7B's architecture and training recipe are derived from Olmo-3-1025-7B (same TransformerConfig.olmo3_7B base, same Dolma 3 pretraining mix, same midtraining yaml as 32B sibling). The hybrid replaces 3-of-4 attention layers with GDN to study GDN+attention scaling vs the pure transformer baseline.

Architecture is built off `TransformerConfig.olmo3_7B(...)` and explicitly described as based on OLMo3 7B but with 2 fewer attention heads (30 vs 32) and reduced d_model so params/TPS match the pure-transformer baseline; trained from scratch (not weight-initialized from OLMo3 7B).
Hybrid base architecture is anchored to OLMo-3 7B with reduced attention heads to match params/TPS for fair comparison; not trained_from but used as architectural scaffold/comparison anchor.

Architecture is built off the olmo3_7B config (32 layers, 4096 d_model) with REMOVE_HEADS=2 (30 heads, 3840 d_model) and a 3:1 Gated-Delta-Net + attention block pattern. No weight inheritance — Hybrid is trained from scratch on the same Dolma 3 0925 mix as OLMo-3 32B.

OLMo-Hybrid-7B is architecturally derived from OLMo3 7B (config olmo3_7B with attention heads reduced from 32 to 30 and d_model adjusted to keep params/TPS comparable for fair-comparison ablation against the pure-transformer OLMo 3 7B).

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B
edges.description: The Dolma 3 web pipeline 'closely follows that of DCLM' for heuristic filtering and quality classification, uses Resiliparse extraction following DCLM, and starts mixing experiments from the DCLM-Baseline mix; methodology is borrowed without DCLM data wholly entering training.
description_variants: The Dolma 3 web pipeline 'closely follows that of DCLM' for heuristic filtering and quality classification, uses Resiliparse extraction following DCLM, and starts mixing experiments from the DCLM-Baseline mix; methodology is borrowed without DCLM data wholly entering training.

The Dolma 3 web pipeline closely follows DCLM: Resiliparse text extraction, the DCLM heuristic-filter cascade, and a fastText quality classifier with positives mirroring DCLM (OpenHermes-2.5, ELI5) and 30GB negatives sampled from DCLM-RefinedWeb. The first round of conditional mixing was performed directly on DCLM-Baseline as a proxy.

allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B's web-data text-extraction and heuristic-filtering pipeline closely follows the DCLM-Baseline recipe; OLMo 3 uses Resiliparse extraction directly from DCLM-pool where available.

Olmo 3 web-data preparation closely follows DCLM (Li et al., 2024a): the team reuses Resiliparse-extracted text from DCLM-pool and follows DCLM heuristic-filtering procedures, and the early DCLM-Baseline mix served as the substrate for the constrained-mixing development.

DCLM (Li et al., 2024a) was the methodological inspiration for Olmo 3's web text pipeline (extraction, heuristic filtering, quality classifier examples) and the first round of conditional mixing was performed over the DCLM Baseline mix.

First-round constrained data mixing was performed on DCLM-Baseline before transferring learned preferences to Olmo 3's bespoke web data; DCLM-Baseline thus seeded the topic-mixture optimization for Olmo 3 7B.
Dolma 3 Mix re-uses the Resiliparse-extracted DCLM-baseline (DCLM-pool) where available, and the initial mixing rounds were performed over the DCLM-Baseline mix.

Olmo 3 Base 7B's Common Crawl heuristic filtering and quality classifier training closely follow DCLM (Li et al., 2024); raw Resiliparse-extracted data is taken directly from DCLM-pool where applicable.
Olmo 3 7B's CommonCrawl pipeline closely follows DCLM's heuristic and quality-filtering recipe and reuses DCLM-pool Resiliparse extractions where available.

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO
edges.description: On-policy preference completions for the 8B mixture were generated by sampling from the 8B Tülu 3 SFT model itself (one of 22 models in the response-generation pool).
description_variants: On-policy preference completions for the 8B mixture were generated by sampling from the 8B Tülu 3 SFT model itself (one of 22 models in the response-generation pool).
On-policy preference responses (one of two responses per prompt) were generated by the Tulu 3 8B SFT model itself, then judged against off-policy generations.

allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT produced on-policy completions used in the chosen/rejected pairs of the 8B preference mixture for allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO (Stage 2 of the synthetic preference pipeline).
On-policy completions in the Tülu 3 8B preference mix are sampled from allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT per the synthetic preference pipeline in Section 5.2.1 'Response Generation'.

On-policy completions used in this preference mix (the SFT-Reused-On-Policy and WildChat-Reused-On-Policy splits) were sampled from allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT itself, then paired against off-policy completions from the model pool.
On-policy preference completions: one of the four candidates per prompt was sampled from Tülu 3 8B SFT (the SFT model that initializes this DPO run).
On-policy preference completions were generated by the Tulu 3 8B SFT model — paired against off-policy completions from external instruct models in the synthetic preference pipeline.

On-policy completions for allenai/llama-3.1-tulu-3-8b-preference-mixture were sampled from allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT (the Tülu 3 8B SFT model) and paired against off-policy outputs to produce the chosen/rejected pairs for DPO.

Tülu 3 8B SFT generates the on-policy half of preference pairs (Stage 2 of §5.2.1): for the on-policy subset, one of the two responses is sampled from this SFT model and the other from an off-policy pool model.

The 8B Tülu 3 SFT checkpoint generated the on-policy slice (one response per pair) of the preference data used to train allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO; the other response comes from one of the off-policy pool models.

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO
edges.description: 8B DPO's preference data construction pipeline (model-pool sampling + GPT-4o judge) is adapted from the UltraFeedback recipe.
description_variants: 8B DPO's preference data construction pipeline (model-pool sampling + GPT-4o judge) is adapted from the UltraFeedback recipe.

Tülu 3's synthetic preference pipeline (4 completions per prompt sampled from a 22-model pool, LLM-as-a-judge ratings across 4 aspects, mean-rating binarization) is an extension of the UltraFeedback pipeline.

The Tulu 3 synthetic preference data pipeline (4-completion sampling + LLM-as-a-judge + binarization) extends the UltraFeedback pipeline of Cui et al. (2023), updating its model pool, integrating on-policy SFT completions, and adding skill-targeted prompt sources.
The Tülu 3 synthetic preference-data pipeline (Figure 7, Section 5.2.1) extends and improves the UltraFeedback recipe by Cui et al. 2023.
Tulu 3 8B DPO preference-data construction follows the UltraFeedback recipe — sample 4 completions per prompt, GPT-4-family LLM-as-judge rates each 1-5 on 4 aspects, binarize by mean rating.

The Tülu 3 preference-data pipeline (prompt selection -> 4-response generation from a model pool -> LLM-as-judge annotation) is an adaptation of the UltraFeedback pipeline (Cui et al., 2023), with updates such as a refreshed model pool and on-policy completions.
Tulu 3 8B DPO's preference pipeline extends the UltraFeedback methodology (Cui et al. 2023) with a refreshed model pool, on-policy completions, and a GPT-4o-based 4-aspect judge.

Tülu 3's synthetic preference data pipeline (Section 5.2.1) extends and improves the UltraFeedback off-policy preference data generation method (Cui et al., 2023); allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO's preference mix derives from this pipeline.

allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO's preference annotations are produced with the Ultrafeedback four-aspect annotation template originally introduced by openbmb/UltraFeedback; the methodology is borrowed but a separate (unnamed) LLM judge is used.

Tülu 3 8B DPO uses a synthetic preference pipeline adapted from UltraFeedback (Cui et al. 2023): same prompt-selection / 4-model-pool sampling / LLM-as-judge binarization template, with refreshed pool and added on-policy data.

Preference annotations in tulu-3-sft-prompts-ultrafeedback use the four-aspect UltraFeedback template (originally from openbmb/UltraFeedback) with an unnamed LLM judge; consumed by Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO during DPO post-training.

The Tülu 3 synthetic preference pipeline that built allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO's preference data extends Cui et al. (2023)'s UltraFeedback recipe: sample N responses from a model pool, then have an LLM judge rate them on multiple aspects.

description_variants: IF-Persona-SFT and IF-augmented constraint definitions are borrowed from IFEval's 25 verifiable constraint types (Zhou et al. 2023).
If-Persona-Sft (29,980 instances in the Tulu 3 SFT mix consumed by Tulu 3 8B SFT) was generated to cover the 25 IFEval constraint types.

The verifiable-constraint taxonomy used in personas-instruction-following (consumed by Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-SFT) was borrowed from the IFEval dataset's constraint taxonomy.

The 25 verifiable-constraint types used as seeds for Tülu 3 Persona IF generation are drawn from the IFEval benchmark (Zhou et al. 2023); subject is Tülu 3 8B SFT, the consumer of the resulting SFT data.
The Persona IF split's verifiable constraints are borrowed from the IFEval constraint taxonomy (Zhou et al. 2023), shaping the instruction-following data used to train this model.

The verifiable-constraint taxonomy used to construct allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following (1-3 constraints per prompt) is borrowed from IFEval; 8B Tülu 3 SFT trained on this data and therefore inherits IFEval's constraint taxonomy as a methodology dependency without IFEval data entering training.

edges.description: Decontamination filter: IFEval evaluation prompts were used to remove overlapping training instances from the SFT mix (8-gram match, prompts only).

anchor_list.excerpt: We consider a training set to be contaminated if any number of its instances overlap with more than 2% of the instances in any of the evaluations in our development and unseen suites. We remove all the training sets that were contaminated
anchor_list.explanation: Section 3.2 documents the cross-evaluation decontamination scheme — IFEval (and other dev/unseen evals) effectively functioned as a contamination filter on the SFT mix.

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO
edges.description: UltraFeedback original (full pipeline) and regenerated through the Tülu 3 synthetic pipeline served as ablation comparator for the 8B DPO.
description_variants: UltraFeedback original (full pipeline) and regenerated through the Tülu 3 synthetic pipeline served as ablation comparator for the 8B DPO.
Tulu 3 8B DPO ablation: trained on UltraFeedback alone vs the team's preference mix to demonstrate the mix outperforms UltraFeedback.
Tulu 3 8B DPO ablation: original UltraFeedback completions vs. completions regenerated by the Tulu 3 synthetic preference pipeline (regenerated outperforms).
UltraFeedback (Cui et al. 2023) is the fixed training set used in the DPO-algorithm ablation that selected length-normalized DPO over SimPO/DPO/PPO for Tülu 3.
Internal ablation: the Tulu 3 8B SFT was DPO-trained on UltraFeedback alone as a baseline against which the Tulu 3 best preference mix was shown to win (+1.8 avg at 8B, +3.3 at 70B).

The DPO/SimPO/PPO algorithm-selection ablation was run on top of an early Tulu 3 SFT checkpoint using UltraFeedback as the preference data, leading to the choice of length-normalized DPO for the final 8B/70B/405B DPO runs.
Tülu 3 8B DPO preference-mix ablation: 'UltraFeedback (UF; original)' was added/removed across mix variants (Table 16) to identify the best 8B preference mix.

PPO ablation: trained reward model on UltraFeedback and ran PPO with the same prompts as the DPO mix; PPO reached comparable but slightly lower scores than DPO and was 7× more expensive (Section 5.4.1 + Table 18 + Figure 16).
UltraFeedback alone was an ablation baseline against the Tulu 3 best preference mix at 8B and 70B (Figure 12); Tulu 3 mix beat it by +1.8 (8B) / +3.3 (70B).

UltraFeedback was used as an alternative DPO training mixture in the 'Going Beyond Ultrafeedback' ablation: the Tülu 3 best mix outperformed DPO trained on UltraFeedback on both 8B and 70B (Figure 12).

anchor_list.excerpt: DPO w. Ultra-Feedback ... we are able to significantly surpass DPO training on UltraFeedback by training on our best mix
anchor_list.explanation: Section 5.3 + Figures 12/15 use UltraFeedback as design-space variant.

anchor_list.excerpt: we were able to significantly surpass DPO training on UltraFeedback by training on our best mix
anchor_list.explanation: UltraFeedback used as ablation comparator.

anchor_list.excerpt: the downstream DPO performance of the regenerated dataset is better than the original dataset
anchor_list.explanation: UltraFeedback regen vs original ablation.

anchor_list.excerpt: We use UltraFeedback as the training dataset in all cases
anchor_list.explanation: Names UltraFeedback as the ablation training set.

anchor_list.excerpt: Effect of different DPO mixes on 8B and 70B models: UltraFeedback, Helpsteer2, and our best preference mix.
anchor_list.explanation: Figure 12 names UltraFeedback as one of the three preference mixes ablated in Tulu 3's own development.

anchor_list.excerpt: We use UltraFeedback as the training dataset in all cases
anchor_list.explanation: Section 5.4.1 names UltraFeedback as the ablation dataset for selecting length-normalized DPO.
Table 16 enumerates Tülu 3 preference-mix ablations including 'UltraFeedback (UF; original)' as a candidate component.

anchor_list.excerpt: For PPO, we train reward models on UltraFeedback and reuse prompts during online training
anchor_list.explanation: Paper documents UltraFeedback-trained RM + PPO as an ablation alternative to length-normalized DPO.

anchor_list.excerpt: improvement is greater for the 70B model (+3.3 vs. +1.8)
anchor_list.explanation: Figure 12 + Section 5.3 quantify the Tulu 3 mix vs. UltraFeedback-only ablation.

anchor_list.excerpt: In Figure 12 we show that we were able to significantly surpass DPO training on UltraFeedback by training on our best mix. ... Helpsteer2 Wang et al. (2024d), another high-quality preference dataset, also performs lower than our best mix on the 8B model.
anchor_list.explanation: UltraFeedback and HelpSteer2 were used as alternative DPO mixtures whose downstream performance was compared with the Tülu 3 best mix (Figure 12).

anchor_list.excerpt: In Figure 12 we show that we were able to significantly surpass DPO training on UltraFeedback by training on our best mix. ... Helpsteer2 Wang et al. (2024d), another high-quality preference dataset, also performs lower than our best mix on the 8B model.

edges.subject: CohereLabs/aya-101
edges.description: CohereLabs/aya-101 (the 13B multilingual instruction model from the Aya open-science initiative) trains on the Aya Dataset, a 204K-pair human-annotated multilingual instruction-tuning corpus covering 65 languages with both original annotations and human re-annotations of automatically generated content.
description_variants: CohereLabs/aya-101 (the 13B multilingual instruction model from the Aya open-science initiative) trains on the Aya Dataset, a 204K-pair human-annotated multilingual instruction-tuning corpus covering 65 languages with both original annotations and human re-annotations of automatically generated content.
The Aya Dataset (human-annotated multilingual instruction data) is one of five training datasets used to finetune Aya-101.

Aya 101 is instruction-tuned on the Aya Dataset, a 204,114-instance human-annotated multilingual IFT corpus covering 65 languages curated via the Aya Annotation Platform (138,844 original annotations + 65,270 re-annotations), released under Apache 2.0.

anchor_list.excerpt: The `Aya Dataset` is a multilingual instruction fine-tuning dataset curated by an open-science community via Aya Annotation Platform from Cohere Labs. The dataset contains a total of 204k human-annotated prompt-completion pairs ... This dataset can be used to train, finetune, and evaluate multilingual LLMs.
anchor_list.explanation: The dataset card explicitly positions Aya Dataset as instruction fine-tuning data for multilingual LLMs in the Aya initiative; CohereLabs/aya-101 is the Aya project's released instruction-tuned checkpoint that consumes this data.

anchor_list.excerpt: The Aya model is trained on the following datasets: ... - Aya Dataset
anchor_list.explanation: Names the Aya Dataset as a training data source, with HF link to CohereLabs/aya_dataset.
anchor_list.position: Model Details > Data Sources, bullet list

anchor_list.excerpt: The Aya Dataset includes all original annotations and a subset of all re-annotations. ... Aya Dataset Total 204,114
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms the size and composition of the Aya Dataset that is used as Aya 101 instruction-tuning data.
anchor_list.position: Section 2.3 'Criteria for Inclusion in Aya Dataset' and Table 2

anchor_list.excerpt: It also includes the Aya Dataset which is the most extensive human-annotated, multilingual, instruction fine-tuning dataset to date. It contains approximately 204,000 rare human curated annotations by fluent speakers in 67 languages
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms the Aya Dataset is part of the instruction fine-tuning data released with the Aya model.
anchor_list.position: Section 'Trained on the Most Extensive Multilingual Dataset to Date'

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO
edges.description: Prompts in the allenai/tulu-3-IF-augmented-on-policy-8b sub-corpus — a constituent of the allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO DPO mixture — are augmented with verifiable constraints sourced from google/IFEval.
description_variants: Prompts in the allenai/tulu-3-IF-augmented-on-policy-8b sub-corpus — a constituent of the allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO DPO mixture — are augmented with verifiable constraints sourced from google/IFEval.

Prompts in this IF-augmented preference split are augmented with verifiable instruction-following constraints drawn from google/IFEval; the constraints become part of the prompt that allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO trains on.

Constraints layered onto Llama 3.1 Tulu 3 IF-Augmented (on-policy 8B)'s prompts were drawn from google/IFEval's verifiable-instruction templates; allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO therefore consumed IFEval-derived constraint text during DPO training.

allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO indirectly trained on prompts from google/IFEval via the allenai/tulu-3-IF-augmented-on-policy-8b preference dataset (prompt source for this preference subset). Constraints from IFEval used to augment prompts.

The Tulu-3-DPO 8B IF-RLVR run also trains on prompts using IFEval's 25 verifiable constraints (with extended variable ranges) as in-domain training constraints, in addition to the IFTrain constraints.

anchor_list.explanation: Source states constraints applied to the prompts come from google/IFEval.
Source explicitly cites google/IFEval as the origin of the verifiable instruction constraints injected into the prompts.

Card states the preference dataset's prompts originate from google/IFEval.

edges.subject: nvidia/llama-3_1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1
edges.description: Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1 was post-trained on the v1.1 Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset (SFT splits: code, math, science, chat, safety; RL split: instruction_following; ~30M+ samples covering 22M math / 10M code / 709k science / 56k IF / 40k chat / 31k safety).
description_variants: Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1 was post-trained on the v1.1 Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset (SFT splits: code, math, science, chat, safety; RL split: instruction_following; ~30M+ samples covering 22M math / 10M code / 709k science / 56k IF / 40k chat / 31k safety).

Ultra-253B (nvidia/Llama-3_1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1) was post-trained on the Llama-Nemotron Post-Training Dataset v1.1 — a release containing SFT data (math, code, science, chat, safety) and RL data (instruction following) totaling roughly 33M samples — released by NVIDIA in support of this model.
Aggregator-level edge: v1's multi-stage post-training (SFT + GRPO RL) drew on the Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset, which NVIDIA released alongside the model to promote openness.

LN-Ultra is post-trained on the open-released nvidia/Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset (~30M samples spanning math, code, science, chat, instruction-following, safety) — the curated SFT/RL aggregator covering reasoning-on and reasoning-off data.
Ultra-253B-v1 was post-trained on data from the publicly released Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset (linked in the card under Training Datasets) covering math/code/reasoning/instruction-following.
Llama-Nemotron Ultra (Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1) was SFT+RL-trained on Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset.

anchor_list.excerpt: We are releasing an additional 2.2M Math and 500K Code Reasoning Data in support of our release of [Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1]... This dataset is a compilation of SFT and RL data that supports improvements of math, code, general reasoning, and instruction following capabilities

edges.subject: nvidia/llama-3_1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1
edges.description: DeepSeek-R1 synthetically generated 3,934,627 responses (the dominant reasoning-on chain-of-thought source) in the post-training dataset Ultra-253B-v1 was trained on.
description_variants: DeepSeek-R1 synthetically generated 3,934,627 responses (the dominant reasoning-on chain-of-thought source) in the post-training dataset Ultra-253B-v1 was trained on.

Synthetic responses generated by DeepSeek-R1 (3,934,627 samples — reasoning traces) are part of the Llama-Nemotron Post-Training Dataset that Ultra-253B was trained on. The dataset documents this generator under 'Models that were used in the creation of this dataset', with some prompts containing responses for both reasoning-on and reasoning-off modes to teach the model to distinguish between the two.
DeepSeek-R1 generates 16 'reasoning' solutions per AoPS problem for the math SFT data LN-Ultra consumes (reasoning-on traces).

DeepSeek-R1 generates the Python (and selected C++) solutions for LN-Ultra's code-reasoning SFT data via Nucleus Sampling (temperature 0.6, top-p 0.95) with reasoning enclosed in <think> tags; ~488K Python samples were retained.
DeepSeek-R1 generates multiple reasoning traces for every science question in LN-Ultra's SFT slice; for questions without ground-truth answers, majority voting infers the most likely correct answer.

DeepSeek-R1 generates multiple candidate responses per general-domain prompt (open QA / closed QA / extraction / brainstorming) for LN-Ultra's reasoning-on general SFT slice; rejection sampling against a reward model retains the highest-quality.
DeepSeek-R1 generates reasoning-on chain-of-thought solutions (16 generations per problem) for the math SFT data that nvidia/Llama-3_1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1 trains on.

DeepSeek-R1 generates multiple Python (and select C++) reasoning solutions per programming question for the code SFT corpus that nvidia/Llama-3_1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1 trains on; nucleus sampling at temperature 0.6, top-p 0.95.

DeepSeek-R1 generates multiple reasoning-trace solutions for every science question; majority-voted across solutions when ground truth is missing, before entering nvidia/Llama-3_1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1's SFT corpus.

DeepSeek-R1 generates multiple reasoning-on responses for the general-domain prompts that enter nvidia/Llama-3_1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1's SFT corpus; rejection-sampled by Llama-3.1-Nemotron-70B-Reward.

anchor_list.explanation: Names DeepSeek-R1 as a 3.9M-sample response generator in the dataset.
Source's generators table lists deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1 as a model whose synthetic outputs entered the dataset that Ultra-253B trained on.

anchor_list.excerpt: We prompt DeepSeek-R1 (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025) and Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct ... We use 16 generations per problem for DeepSeek-R1

anchor_list.explanation: Paper names DeepSeek-R1 as the code-solution generator.

anchor_list.excerpt: For responses, we prompt DeepSeek-R1 (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025) for multiple generations and perform rejection sampling using the Llama-3.1-Nemotron-70B reward model (NVIDIA, 2024b).
anchor_list.explanation: Paper names DeepSeek-R1 as generator for general-domain reasoning-on responses.

anchor_list.explanation: Documents DeepSeek-R1 as the reasoning-trace generator.

anchor_list.excerpt: We employ DeepSeek-R1 (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025) to generate multiple solutions per question, primarily in Python, with C++ solutions also generated
anchor_list.explanation: DeepSeek-R1 is the code-reasoning solution generator.

anchor_list.excerpt: For all questions in the dataset, we use DeepSeek-R1 (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025) to generate multiple reasoning traces.
anchor_list.explanation: DeepSeek-R1 generates the reasoning-on solutions for science.

anchor_list.excerpt: For responses, we prompt DeepSeek-R1 (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025) for multiple generations and perform rejection sampling using the Llama-3.1-Nemotron-70B reward model
anchor_list.explanation: DeepSeek-R1 is the general-domain reasoning generator.
