edges.subject: nvidia/Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1
edges.description: Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1 was post-trained on the v1.1 Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset (SFT splits: code, math, science, chat, safety; RL split: instruction_following).
description_variants: Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1 was post-trained on the v1.1 Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset (SFT splits: code, math, science, chat, safety; RL split: instruction_following).

Nano-8B (nvidia/Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1) was post-trained on the Llama-Nemotron Post-Training Dataset v1.1 — a release containing SFT data (math, code, science, chat, safety) and RL data (instruction following) totaling roughly 33M samples — released by NVIDIA in support of this model.

LN-Nano is post-trained on the open-released nvidia/Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset (~30M samples spanning math, code, science, chat, instruction-following, safety) — the curated SFT/RL aggregator covering reasoning-on and reasoning-off data.
LN-Nano SFT consumes the released Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset over a three-stage curriculum.

anchor_list.excerpt: in support of NVIDIA's release of ... and [Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1).
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms Nano-8B-v1 is a target consumer of the post-training dataset.

anchor_list.excerpt: This dataset is a compilation of SFT and RL data that supports improvements of math, code, general reasoning, and instruction following capabilities of the original Llama instruct model, in support of NVIDIA's release of [Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B-v1], [Llama-3.3-Nemotron-Super-49B-v1] and [Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1].
anchor_list.explanation: Source explicitly states the dataset supports the post-training of all three Nemotron derivatives, including this subject.

anchor_list.excerpt: we also open-source the Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset, a carefully curated dataset used during the supervised and reinforcement learning stages of training for LN-Nano, LN-Super, and LN-Ultra.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper explicitly states the released dataset was used in SFT/RL of all three LN models.

anchor_list.excerpt: In the first stage, the model is fine-tuned exclusively on reasoning data from code, math, and science domains (Section 3.1) ... In the second stage, we introduce non-reasoning data (Section 3.2) mixed with reasoning samples
anchor_list.explanation: LN-Nano consumes the same post-training data domains across three SFT stages.

edges.subject: nvidia/Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1
edges.description: DeepSeek-R1 synthetically generated 3,934,627 responses (the dominant reasoning-on chain-of-thought source) in the post-training dataset Nano-8B-v1 trained on.
description_variants: DeepSeek-R1 synthetically generated 3,934,627 responses (the dominant reasoning-on chain-of-thought source) in the post-training dataset Nano-8B-v1 trained on.

Synthetic responses generated by DeepSeek-R1 (3,934,627 samples — reasoning traces) are part of the Llama-Nemotron Post-Training Dataset that Nano-8B was trained on. The dataset documents this generator under 'Models that were used in the creation of this dataset', with some prompts containing responses for both reasoning-on and reasoning-off modes to teach the model to distinguish between the two.
DeepSeek-R1 generates 16 'reasoning' solutions per AoPS problem for the math SFT data LN-Nano consumes (reasoning-on traces).

DeepSeek-R1 generates the Python (and selected C++) solutions for LN-Nano's code-reasoning SFT data via Nucleus Sampling (temperature 0.6, top-p 0.95) with reasoning enclosed in <think> tags; ~488K Python samples were retained.
DeepSeek-R1 generates multiple reasoning traces for every science question in LN-Nano's SFT slice; for questions without ground-truth answers, majority voting infers the most likely correct answer.

DeepSeek-R1 generates multiple candidate responses per general-domain prompt (open QA / closed QA / extraction / brainstorming) for LN-Nano's reasoning-on general SFT slice; rejection sampling against a reward model retains the highest-quality.
DeepSeek-R1 generates reasoning-on chain-of-thought solutions (16 generations per problem) for the math SFT data that nvidia/Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1 trains on.

DeepSeek-R1 generates multiple Python (and select C++) reasoning solutions per programming question for the code SFT corpus that nvidia/Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1 trains on; nucleus sampling at temperature 0.6, top-p 0.95.

DeepSeek-R1 generates multiple reasoning-trace solutions for every science question; majority-voted across solutions when ground truth is missing, before entering nvidia/Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1's SFT corpus.
DeepSeek-R1 generates multiple reasoning-on responses for the general-domain prompts that enter nvidia/Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Nano-8B-v1's SFT corpus; rejection-sampled by Llama-3.1-Nemotron-70B-Reward.

edges.subject: allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO
edges.description: The pref-IF preference dataset is constructed by rewriting prompts from allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following to relax one constraint per prompt, so the dataset's design is directly derived from the SFT version's constraint-grounded prompts.
description_variants: The pref-IF preference dataset is constructed by rewriting prompts from allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following to relax one constraint per prompt, so the dataset's design is directly derived from the SFT version's constraint-grounded prompts.

The preference dataset allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO consumed was constructed by reworking prompts from the SFT-stage allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following dataset; methodology borrowed from that SFT recipe (rewrite to relax one constraint to produce a rejected response).

The preference dataset allenai/tulu-3-pref-personas-instruction-following that allenai/Llama-3.1-Tulu-3-8B-DPO trained on was constructed by taking a subset of the SFT-stage dataset allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following and rewriting prompts to relax one of the given constraints; the response to the modified prompt is treated as the rejected completion. This reuses the persona-IF data recipe at the DPO stage.

anchor_list.excerpt: we took a subset of its supervised-tuning version [here](https://huggingface.co/datasets/allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following) and convert it into preference dataset. Specifically, we rewrote each prompt in the subset to relax one of the given constraints
anchor_list.explanation: Card explicitly states pref-IF was constructed by rewriting prompts from the SFT-personas-IF dataset, indicating methodological derivation.

anchor_list.excerpt: we took a subset of its supervised-tuning version (allenai/tulu-3-sft-personas-instruction-following) and convert it into preference dataset

Stage-1/Stage-2 science SFT prompts include problems from the post-training datasets in Llama-Nemotron (Bercovich et al., 2025; Nathawani et al., 2025). Subject nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-14B-Thinking trained on the resulting science SFT data.

Stage-2 tool-calling SFT data is the tool-calling subset of the Llama-Nemotron post-training dataset (Nathawani et al., 2025); subject nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-14B-Thinking sees 310K conversations / 1.41M turns (responses generated by Qwen3-235B-A22B). Note: this is a distinct role from the science prompt source.

IF-RL prompts come from the Llama-Nemotron instruction-following dataset (NVIDIA, 2025); originally 56K samples reduced to 40K after preprocessing. Subject nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-14B-Thinking trained on this IF-RL data via Nemotron-Cascade-RL-Instruction-Following.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-14B-Thinking
edges.description: DeepSeek-R1 verifies math problem answers (up to 8 attempts, majority voting) for the Math-RL data feeding 14B Math RL.
description_variants: DeepSeek-R1 is used as a verifier with up to eight attempts per problem to retain only problems with majority-voted correct answers in NuminaMath; ambiguous or noisy items are discarded before Math RL training.

anchor_list.excerpt: each problem is verified by the DeepSeek-R1 model with up to eight attempts
anchor_list.explanation: DeepSeek-R1 verifier applies to all Math-RL data.

anchor_list.excerpt: Because NuminaMath contains OCR and parsing errors, each problem is verified by the DeepSeek-R1 model with up to eight attempts. A rule-based verifier retains only problems with majority-voted correct answers
anchor_list.explanation: Documents DeepSeek-R1 as the prompt-quality filter.

edges.subject: simplescaling/s1.1-32B
edges.description: The reasoning traces in s1K-1.1 used to train s1.1-32B were generated by DeepSeek-R1 (replacing the Gemini Thinking traces used for s1K). Bespoke Labs assisted with generation via the Curator tool.
description_variants: The reasoning traces in s1K-1.1 used to train s1.1-32B were generated by DeepSeek-R1 (replacing the Gemini Thinking traces used for s1K). Bespoke Labs assisted with generation via the Curator tool.

DeepSeek-R1 generated the reasoning traces for the 1,000 s1K-1.1 samples, replacing Gemini-generated traces from s1K. Trace generation was done with the Bespoke Curator tool by Ryan Marten (acknowledgements).
The reasoning (thinking) traces in simplescaling/s1K-1.1 used to fine-tune s1.1-32B were generated by DeepSeek-R1, replacing the Gemini-2.0-flash-thinking traces used in the original s1K.

DeepSeek-R1 generated the reasoning traces (deepseek_thinking_trajectory and deepseek_attempt fields) for the 1,000 s1K-1.1 questions; s1.1-32B is fine-tuned from Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct on these DeepSeek-R1 traces (rather than the legacy Gemini traces also present in the dataset).

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Instruct-DPO
edges.description: Multi-turn preference contexts in Dolci-Instruct-DPO are synthetically extended from the Tülu 3 DPO dataset (Lambert et al., 2024).
description_variants: Multi-turn preference contexts in Dolci-Instruct-DPO are synthetically extended from the Tülu 3 DPO dataset (Lambert et al., 2024).
Dolci Instruct DPO modernizes the UltraFeedback pipeline from OLMo 2 and Tulu 3 (Lambert et al., 2024) and synthetically extends Tulu 3-DPO prompts for multi-turn preference data.

The multi-turn DPO data is synthetically extended from the Tulu 3-DPO dataset; the UltraFeedback-style GPT-judge pipeline that Olmo 3 Instruct DPO modernizes also originates with Tulu 3 / OLMo 2 work.
Multi-turn DPO data extends the Tulu-3-DPO methodology / dataset with self-talk and synthetic-context multi-turn conversations.

WildChat English contributed 19,403 general-chat prompts (topic and character filtered, English-only subset of WildChat) into the Dolci-Think-Completions-DPO pool, which was filtered into Dolci-Think-RL-7B for Olmo-3-7B-Think RLVR training.
6,421 WildChat English prompts (filtered for reasoning suitability) contribute to the General RLVR Mixture of Dolci-Think-RL-7B used to RL-train Olmo-3-7B-Think.

6,421 prompts sourced from WildChat English are mixed into allenai/Dolci-Think-RL-7B (the RL dataset that trains allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think); the parent mix is filtered with execution-based test-case validation, F1-rewrite filtering, Nemotron difficulty-tier selection, dedup and constraint normalization before RL.

6,421 prompts from WildChat English were included in the Dolci-Think-RL mixture used to RL post-train allenai/Olmo-3-7B-Think; WildChat English split filtered for reasoning suitability (paper arXiv:2405.01470).

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B
edges.description: allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B's heuristic filtering and HTML-text extraction pipeline closely follow those of DCLM (Li et al., 2024a), and DCLM-pool's Resiliparse-extracted data is directly leveraged where applicable.
description_variants: allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B's heuristic filtering and HTML-text extraction pipeline closely follow those of DCLM (Li et al., 2024a), and DCLM-pool's Resiliparse-extracted data is directly leveraged where applicable.
Heuristic filtering and FastText quality classifier training closely follow DCLM (Li et al., 2024a), with minor modifications to improve data quality and computational efficiency.

DCLM's text-extraction pipeline (Resiliparse on WARC files) and heuristic filtering recipe were adopted (with minor modifications) for Olmo 3 7B's web data; team also directly reused DCLM-pool's Resiliparse extractions for dumps already covered. Quality classifier training also follows DCLM's choice of positive/negative examples.
Dolma 3 Mix's web-data preparation (text extraction, heuristic filtering, deduplication, MADLAD-style sentence rules) closely follows the DCLM recipe.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-32B-Think
edges.description: Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT generated 5,031,398 completions over 636,095 candidate prompts (Dolci-Think-RL-7B-Completions-SFT); rollout pass-rates were used to filter easy prompts from the shared candidate pool from which Dolci-Think-RL-32B (the RLVR training mixture for Olmo-3-32B-Think) was drawn.
description_variants: Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT generated 5,031,398 completions over 636,095 candidate prompts (Dolci-Think-RL-7B-Completions-SFT); rollout pass-rates were used to filter easy prompts from the shared candidate pool from which Dolci-Think-RL-32B (the RLVR training mixture for Olmo-3-32B-Think) was drawn.

Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT generated completions over the candidate prompt pool considered when making Dolci-Think-RL (both 7B and 32B variants); pass-rates were used to filter 'easy' prompts during construction of Dolci-Think-RL-32B.

anchor_list.explanation: Completions-SFT filtered prompts from the Dolci-Think-RL candidate pool, which feeds both 7B and 32B Think RL stages.

anchor_list.excerpt: Dolci-Think-Completions-SFT is a set of 5,031,398 completions(!!) from the Olmo-3-7B-Think-SFT model over the prompts considered when making Dolci-Think-RL. These completions were mainly used to filter easy data

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B
edges.description: the-stack-v2 (Lozhkov et al., 2024) is the upstream GitHub-repository corpus that Stack-Edu (the direct code data source for Olmo 3 7B) re-curates with educational programming filtering.
description_variants: the-stack-v2 (Lozhkov et al., 2024) is the upstream GitHub-repository corpus that Stack-Edu (the direct code data source for Olmo 3 7B) re-curates with educational programming filtering.
Olmo 3 Base 7B's CraneCode midtraining sub-corpus sources Python from the-stack-v2-smol (smol variant of bigcode/the-stack-v2) before applying SwallowCode rewriting.

CraneCode (a midtraining sub-corpus of allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B) sources Python files from The Stack v2 Smol subset, then applies syntax/lint filtering and Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct two-stage rewriting.

anchor_list.excerpt: we use Stack-Edu (Allal et al., 2025), an improved curation of GitHub repositories from the-stack-v2 dataset (Lozhkov et al., 2024)
anchor_list.explanation: Section 3.4.3 names the-stack-v2 as the upstream source from which Stack-Edu is derived; captures the indirect lineage feeding Olmo 3 pretraining.

anchor_list.excerpt: we source data from the Python subset of the-stack-v2-smol
anchor_list.explanation: the-stack-v2 supplies the Python source data for CraneCode consumed by Olmo 3 Base 7B.

anchor_list.excerpt: rewriting FineMath4+ with the SwallowMath prompt ... we collect the Megamath-Web-Pro data occurring after June 2023 ... we created a fully-open replica of SwallowCode ... starting with just the python files from The Stack v2 Smol ... we adopt the TinyGSM protocol (Liu et al., 2023a) and prompts to generate 100 new problems for each existing MATH problem
anchor_list.explanation: Names the raw source datasets that synthetic midtraining sub-corpora are derived from.
anchor_list.position: Section 3.5.2 Math/Code capabilities, pages 23-24; Appendix A.3.1, page 92

anchor_list.excerpt: we applied a two-stage rewriting process, first to generate code data that is more compliant to the python style guides (SGCR), and then to generate optimized code (SCOR); both using the prompts from the original SwallowCode paper and Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct (Qwen et al., 2024).
anchor_list.explanation: Identifies Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct as the rewriter producing CraneCode.

edges.subject: allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B
edges.description: Qwen3 was used as the rewriter that produced the MegaMatt synthetic-math midtraining sub-corpus consumed by allenai/Olmo-3-1025-7B (filtered Megamath-Web-Pro post-June-2023 rewritten with Qwen3).

anchor_list.excerpt: we re-generate this dataset using open source models. Specifically, we collect the Megamath-Web-Pro data occurring after June 2023, apply filtering as in Megamath-Web-Pro-Max, and rewrite it using Qwen3 (Yang et al., 2025a). This yields 3.88B tokens of high-quality data, which we refer to as MegaMatt.
anchor_list.explanation: States Qwen3 was the rewriter used to produce MegaMatt from filtered Megamath-Web-Pro.

edges.description: Through the Nemotron-Cascade-RL-Instruction-Following dataset, Nemotron-Cascade-8B trains on prompts drawn from a filtered and pre-processed RL split of nvidia/Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset.
description_variants: Through the Nemotron-Cascade-RL-Instruction-Following dataset, Nemotron-Cascade-8B trains on prompts drawn from a filtered and pre-processed RL split of nvidia/Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset.

Stage-1/Stage-2 science SFT prompts include problems from the post-training datasets in Llama-Nemotron (Bercovich et al., 2025; Nathawani et al., 2025). Subject nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B trained on the resulting science SFT data.

Stage-2 tool-calling SFT data is the tool-calling subset of the Llama-Nemotron post-training dataset (Nathawani et al., 2025); subject nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B sees 310K conversations / 1.41M turns (responses generated by Qwen3-235B-A22B). Note: this is a distinct role from the science prompt source.

IF-RL prompts come from the Llama-Nemotron instruction-following dataset (NVIDIA, 2025); originally 56K samples reduced to 40K after preprocessing. Subject nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B trained on this IF-RL data via Nemotron-Cascade-RL-Instruction-Following.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B
edges.description: Through nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-RL-Math, Nemotron-Cascade-8B trains on 424 math problems sourced from nvidia/OpenMathReasoning.
description_variants: Through nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-RL-Math, Nemotron-Cascade-8B trains on 424 math problems sourced from nvidia/OpenMathReasoning.

nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B trained on prompts from nvidia/OpenMathReasoning as part of the Stage-1 SFT math domain (270,534 questions / 2,147,570 samples). The nvidia/OpenMathReasoning prompts were paired with DeepSeek-R1-generated reasoning responses inside the Nemotron-Cascade-SFT-Stage-1 blend.

nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B trained on prompts from nvidia/OpenMathReasoning as part of the Stage-2 SFT math domain (311,505 questions / 1,877,122 samples). The nvidia/OpenMathReasoning prompts were paired with thinking responses from DeepSeek-R1-0528 and/or non-thinking responses from DeepSeek-V3 / DeepSeek-V3-0324 in the Stage-2 blend.

Stage-1 math SFT prompts include problems from OpenMathReasoning (Moshkov et al., 2025). Subject nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B trained on this data via the Nemotron-Cascade-SFT-Stage-1 / SFT-Stage-2 mix.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B
edges.description: GPQA-Diamond reported as Pass@1 in the Nemotron-Cascade-8B release eval table at temperature 0.6, top-p 0.95, max-output 64K (YaRN factor 2.0).
description_variants: GPQA-Diamond reported as Pass@1 in the Nemotron-Cascade-8B release eval table at temperature 0.6, top-p 0.95, max-output 64K (YaRN factor 2.0).

GPQA-Diamond (Rein et al., 2024): 198 highest-quality graduate-level physics/biology/chemistry questions from GPQA; pass@1 averaged over 8 generations in thinking mode. Specifically the diamond split of Idavidrein/gpqa. Subject nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B is the evaluated final release.

anchor_list.explanation: Row in the eval table reports GPQA-Diamond Pass@1 under the Nemotron-Cascade-8B column.

anchor_list.excerpt: GPQA-Diamond (Rein et al., 2024) is a benchmark for assessing an LLM's scientific reasoning capability. It consists of the highest quality 198 GPQA questions
anchor_list.explanation: Names GPQA as a benchmark in the Nemotron-Cascade evaluation suite reported in Table 1.

nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B trained on prompts from nvidia/OpenCodeReasoning as part of the Stage-2 SFT code domain (34,747 questions / 1,143,266 samples). The nvidia/OpenCodeReasoning prompts were paired with thinking responses from DeepSeek-R1-0528 and/or non-thinking responses from DeepSeek-V3 / DeepSeek-V3-0324 in the Stage-2 blend.

Stage-1 and Stage-2 code SFT prompts include problems from OpenCodeReasoning (Ahmad et al., 2025); Stage-2 uses OpenCodeReasoning as a primary prompt source. Subject nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-8B trained on this data via the Stage-1/Stage-2 SFT mix.

edges.subject: THUDM/LongAlign-6B-64k
edges.description: THUDM/LongAlign-6B-64k is supervised-fine-tuned on the 10k bilingual long-context instruction-tuning dataset THUDM/LongAlign-10k (8k-64k tokens per example, 10% Chinese), mixed with ShareGPT short instructions.
description_variants: THUDM/LongAlign-6B-64k is supervised-fine-tuned on the 10k bilingual long-context instruction-tuning dataset THUDM/LongAlign-10k (8k-64k tokens per example, 10% Chinese), mixed with ShareGPT short instructions.

LongAlign-6B-64k is supervised fine-tuned on the LongAlign-10k dataset, a bilingual (English and Chinese) collection of 10,000 long-context instructions ranging from 8k to 64k tokens, using the LongAlign recipe with packing and loss weighting.

LongAlign-6B-64k is the SFT chat model produced by LongAlign training, which uses the proposed LongAlign-10k bilingual (English/Chinese) long instruction dataset of 10k examples ranging 8k-64k tokens.

THUDM/LongAlign-6B-64k was supervised-fine-tuned on the LongAlign-10k long-instruction dataset (10,000 instances of 8k-64k length, including ~10% Chinese), mixed with ShareGPT short-instruction data using packing with loss weighting on LongAlign-10k mixed with ShareGPT.

anchor_list.excerpt: we create tasks and answers for 10k lengthy texts, yielding a total of 10k instances of supervised data, of which 10% is in Chinese. The length of these data ranges from 8k to 64k

edges.subject: Goedel-LM/Goedel-Prover-V2-32B
edges.description: Goedel-Prover-V2-32B's weights are initialized from Qwen3-32B and continued via expert-iteration plus RL post-training with scaffolded data synthesis, verifier-guided self-correction, and checkpoint averaging.
description_variants: Goedel-Prover-V2-32B's weights are initialized from Qwen3-32B and continued via expert-iteration plus RL post-training with scaffolded data synthesis, verifier-guided self-correction, and checkpoint averaging.
Goedel-Prover-V2-32B is fine-tuned from Qwen3-32B as the base model for the V2 prover series.

anchor_list.explanation: HF model card frontmatter standardly declares the base/init checkpoint of a derived model; here it names Qwen/Qwen3-32B as the base for the 32B Goedel prover.

anchor_list.excerpt: We employ S1 to conduct SFT on both Qwen3-8B and Qwen3-32B, resulting in fine-tuned models SFT-S1 for both the 8B and 32B variants.
anchor_list.explanation: States Qwen3-32B is the base checkpoint that the Goedel-Prover-V2 32B SFT pipeline starts from.

edges.subject: Goedel-LM/Goedel-Prover-V2-32B
edges.description: Qwen3-32B drives informal-based scaffolded data synthesis: for each problem it produces simpler/sub-problems (when unsolved) or harder variants (when already solved), plus an attempted natural-language solution used as context. These generated informal problems are then formalized and added to S3.

anchor_list.excerpt: For a given problem, we prompt an LLM (Qwen3-32B) to generate simpler/sub-problems if it is unsolved, or harder variants if it is already solved.
anchor_list.explanation: Identifies Qwen3-32B as the generator of the scaffolded informal problems that, after formalization, become training data for the 32B prover.
anchor_list.position: Section 2.2 Informal-based scaffolded data synthesis, page 4

edges.subject: Goedel-LM/Goedel-Prover-V2-32B
edges.description: Qwen3-32B serves as the LLM judge that filters generated formal statements for correctness and difficulty (4 majority-vote inferences per formalization) in the negation-and-difficulty filtering stage of the scaffolded data synthesis pipeline used to build S3.

anchor_list.excerpt: For each formalization, we query Qwen3-32B for 4 times, where each inference judges the correctness and the simplicity simultaneously. The final correctness is judged by strict majority voting among the 4 judges.
anchor_list.explanation: Appendix D explicitly documents Qwen3-32B as the inclusion-decision filter for synthesized formal statements feeding the 32B prover's S3 training data.
anchor_list.position: Appendix D, page 23, item 3 Negation and difficulty filtering

edges.subject: Goedel-LM/Goedel-Prover-V2-32B
edges.description: Goedel-Formalizer-V2 (32B) translates informal natural-language problems generated by Qwen3-32B into Lean 4 formal statements; these formalized statements feed the scaffolded dataset S3 that trains the 32B prover.
description_variants: Goedel-Formalizer-V2 (32B) translates informal natural-language problems generated by Qwen3-32B into Lean 4 formal statements; these formalized statements feed the scaffolded dataset S3 that trains the 32B prover.

Goedel-Prover-V2-32B's training data includes Lean 4 formal statements that were autoformalized from informal natural-language math problems by the Goedel-Formalizer-V2-32B autoformalizer (the formalizer rewrites NL into Lean 4 with chain-of-thought reasoning).

anchor_list.excerpt: The generated informal statements are then formalized into Lean by the formalization pipeline we described in Formalizer Training.
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms the formalizer rewrites Qwen3-generated informal content into Lean — a content-modifying transform in the prover's training data construction.
anchor_list.position: Section 2.2 Informal-based scaffolded data synthesis, page 4

anchor_list.excerpt: In Goedel-Prover-V2 project we use this formalizer in generating lean4 statements.
anchor_list.explanation: Identifies the 32B formalizer as the autoformalization producer for the Goedel-Prover-V2 project, of which Goedel-Prover-V2-32B is the flagship model.

edges.subject: Goedel-LM/Goedel-Formalizer-V2-32B

anchor_list.excerpt: Goedel-Formalizer-V2-32B is a 32B-parameter autoformalizer (fine-tuned from Qwen3-32B) that translates informal natural-language math problems into Lean 4 formal statements with explicit chain-of-thought reasoning.
